title: To God: On His Sickness.
content:
What though my harp and viol be
    Both hung upon the willow tree?
    What though my bed be now my grave,
    And for my house I darkness have?
    What though my healthful days are fled,
    And I lie number'd with the dead?
    Yet I have hope, by Thy great power,
    To spring; though now a wither'd flower.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Robert Herrick, titled "To God: On His Sickness.", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
